title: Extension hardening
summary: Vortex2+2 now keeps hosted relay launch credentials in the extension layer, stores license settings locally, and narrows extension fetch/resource exposure.
changelog: Moved Play in Browser launch handoff behind an extension-managed nonce instead of URL token parameters.
Moved hosted relay WebSocket ownership into the extension content script so page JS no longer receives the launch token.
Removed the third-party cors.io fetch bridge and restricted bridge fetches to known Vortex/update hosts.
Moved license and relay settings from Chrome Sync to local extension storage.
Restricted web-accessible extension assets and redirect rules to Vortex origins.
Kept existing local relay compatibility while tightening hosted relay credential handling.
